scenario: Orion Insurance evaluates renewal anniversaries on Camunda 8.8 over 4500 policies per day and assembles dates from numeric fields like year=2012, month=12, day=25 on the process instance. A modeller needs the documented behaviour of the date(year, month, day) signature on the conversion functions page, in particular what the function returns when components do not represent a valid calendar date such as 2024,6,31.
question: What does the conversion functions page state date(year, month, day) returns for valid components and for invalid components such as 2024,6,31?

options.text: date(year, month, day) returns the assembled date for valid components and clamps the day to the last day of the month for invalid components, so date(2024, 6, 31) returns date("2024-06-30") and date(2012, 12, 25) returns date("2012-12-25")

date(year, month, day) returns the assembled date for valid components and raises an incident on the process instance for invalid components such as 2024,6,31, so date(2012, 12, 25) returns date("2012-12-25") and date(2024, 6, 31) interrupts the FEEL evaluation

date(year, month, day) returns a FEEL date for valid components and returns null when the components do not represent a valid calendar date, so date(2012, 12, 25) returns date("2012-12-25") and date(2024, 6, 31) returns null on the conversion functions page

date(year, month, day) returns the assembled date for valid components and rolls overflow days into the next month for invalid components, so date(2024, 6, 31) returns date("2024-07-01") and date(2012, 12, 25) returns date("2012-12-25")

a.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document day clamping; the function returns null when components do not represent a valid calendar date such as 2024,6,31, rather than adjusting the day to 30.

b.text: Incorrect. The function does not raise an incident on invalid components; the conversion functions page states it returns null, leaving the FEEL evaluation to continue and the caller to handle the null result.

c.text: Correct. The conversion functions page documents date(year: number, month: number, day: number): date and states that the function returns null if the components do not represent a valid calendar date, citing 2024,6,31 as invalid because June has only 30 days. It shows date(2012, 12, 25) returning date("2012-12-25").

d.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document overflow roll-over; the function returns null when components do not represent a valid calendar date such as 2024,6,31, rather than rolling the day into the next month.

explanation: date(year: number, month: number, day: number): date returns a date from the given components, and returns null if the components do not represent a valid calendar date. The conversion functions page cites 2024,6,31 as invalid because June has only 30 days and shows date(2012, 12, 25) returning date("2012-12-25").
